label: Network Forensics Analyst
description: Investigates security incidents through network traffic analysis — PCAP analysis, protocol reconstruction, intrusion analysis, and network-based threat hunting.

paths.name: Network Forensics Track
paths.rationale: GCIA and GNFA together provide the strongest network forensics credential combination — intrusion analysis paired with advanced network artifact reconstruction.
pros: GCIA and GNFA both DoD 8140 approved
GNFA is the only dedicated network forensics GIAC cert
Strong for NSM, MSSP, and government network analysis roles
cons: Deep TCP/IP knowledge is a prerequisite that takes time to develop

prerequisites.experience: Network administration or SOC analyst background strongly recommended.

cert_sequence.notes: GIAC Certified Intrusion Analyst — network traffic analysis, IDS/IPS tuning, protocol analysis, network-based threat detection. DoD 8140 approved. CyberLive requires actual packet analysis. Wireshark daily practice is essential preparation. Associated with SANS SEC503.

GIAC Network Forensic Analyst — advanced network forensics covering full packet capture analysis, protocol reconstruction (VoIP, email, HTTP/S), artifact recovery, and network timeline reconstruction. Most recognized dedicated network forensics credential. Associated with SANS FOR572.
